title: Cynoglossum littorale
intro: Cynoglossum littorale, also known as Sea Hound's Tongue, is a perennial flowering plant native to the coastal regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Cynoglossum littorale include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sea Hound's Tongue is commonly prepared as an infusion, tincture, or extract, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs.
taxonomy: Cynoglossum littorale belongs to the family Boraginaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-not family. This species is further classified as a member of the genus Cynoglossum, which comprises about 35 species of flowering plants. Cynoglossum littorale is native to the Mediterranean region and is also found in other parts of Europe, Asia, and Africa. As a member of the genus Cynoglossum, Cynoglossum littorale is a perennial herb with blue flowers and a distinctive shape. The classification of Cynoglossum littorale is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, and order Boraginales.
